{{Experimental}} Description of the property for the number of page views of the page for the last 30 days.

{{Experimental}} Description of the property for the number of unique references cited on the page. This property is omitted for file pages and may be null if the reference count cannot be successfully determined (e.g. due to a parsing error).
{{Experimental}} Description of the property for the calls to action information for the page.
{{Experimental}} Description of the property for the donation call to action objects for the page.
{{Experimental}} Description of the property for the default donation call to action URL.
{{Experimental}} Description of the property for the foundation donation call to action URL.
{{Experimental}} Description of the property for the donation call to action URL for the special occasions.
{{Experimental}} Description of the property for the participation call to action objects for the page.
{{Experimental}} Description of the property for the talk page call to action URL.
{{Experimental}} Description of the property for the trending information about the page. This property contains the following sub-properties: top and relative

{{Experimental}} Description of the property that indicated wether the page is among the project’s most read, most edited, or both over the last 24 hours. This property contains the following sub-properties: read, edited and read-and-edited.
{{Experimental}} Description of the property that indicates if the page is in the top 1000 read pages on the wiki.
{{Experimental}} Description of the property that indicates if the page is in the top 100 edited pages on the wiki.
{{Experimental}} Description of the property that indicates if the page is in the top 1000 read pages and top 100 edited pages on the wiki.

{{Experimental}} Description of the propertry that indicates if the page is outperforming its average read and edit rates. This property contains the following sub-properties: read, edited and read-and-edited.
{{Experimental}}  Description of the propertry that indicates if the page is currently exceeding its average read rate.
{{Experimental}}  Description of the propertry that indicates if the page has a higher rate of edits being submitted than its average.
{{Experimental}}  Description of the propertry that indicates if the page has more read and edit activity than its average performance.
